query: ECB banks instruction AI-related cyber collapse risk planning expectations

{"query":"ECB banks instruction AI-related cyber collapse risk planning expectations","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1661,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.jdsupra.com/legalnews/ecb-calls-significant-institutions-to-3406104/","title":"ECB Calls Significant Institutions To Draft An Action Plan Against AI Related Cybersecurity Threats | A&O Shearman - JDSupra","content":"The action plan must be submitted to the respective joint supervisory team (JST) by 31 October after which the JST will discuss the plan with the bank and monitor progress.
In the short term, the ECB expects banks to prioritise vulnerability and patch management, monitoring and detection, AI-enabled defensive capabilities, third-party ICT risk management, and protection of perimeter technologies and externally exposed ICT assets.
The ECB also urges banks to remediate outstanding ICT-related supervisory findings without delay, noting that unresolved weaknesses identified through prior supervisory activity may become increasingly material in the evolving threat landscape.
The ECB confirms that DORA requirements remain highly relevant and that it will extend the deadline for the annual IT Risk Questionnaire from September 2026 to February 2027.","date":"1 day ago"},{"url":"https://kpmg.com/lu/en/insights/risk-and-regulation/ezb-calls-for-an-action-plan-to-combat-AI-driven-cyberattacks.html","title":"ECB calls for an action plan against AI-enabled cyberattacks by 31 October 2026: What matters for banks now | KPMG Luxembourg","content":"The European Central Bank (ECB) now wants financial institutions to provide information in the short term on how well prepared they are for these threats, and is calling for an action plan to be drawn up promptly.
The deadline: 31 October 2026.
The implication is that institutions wishing to defend against AI-driven attacks must make AI itself the cornerstone of their defences.
We consolidate internal and external assets into a single, unified inventory and assess each asset using AI-driven risk scoring based on exposure window and business criticality."},{"url":"https://www.itcpeacademy.org/blog/news-ecb-orders-banks-to-address-ai-cyber-risks","title":"ECB Orders Banks to Address AI Cyber Risks","content":"The European Central Bank has instructed major euro‑area lenders to produce detailed plans for tackling AI‑driven cyber threats, warning that new frontier‑scale models could undermine the resilience of financial infrastructure.
In letters sent to CEOs, the ECB set an end‑of‑October deadline for banks to outline how they will strengthen their defences.
Longer‑term expectations focus on modernising legacy infrastructure, bolstering crisis‑response and recovery processes, and expanding information‑sharing arrangements to help the sector react more quickly to emerging threats."},{"url":"https://www.thewatcherpost.eu/ecs-ai-wake-up-call-for-europes-banks/","title":"ECB’s AI wake-up call for Europe's banks - The Watcher Post","content":"8 days ago — Its message was unusually direct: by 31 October 2026, each institution must submit a detailed action plan explaining how it will prepare for ...Read more
Second, stronger monitoring and intrusion detection, including AI based defensive tools, which the ECB encourages provided they follow a serious assessment of benefits and risks and remain under human oversight.
They must also revisit risk tolerance frameworks, the thresholds a board sets for how much cyber risk it will accept.
To free up banks’ IT resources, the ECB postpones the annual IT risk questionnaire from September 2026 to February 2027 and stands ready to defer, case by case, inspections unrelated to the priority areas.
The European Systemic Risk Board has issued a formal warning on systemic cyber risks from frontier AI models, and convergent alerts have come from UK, Singaporean, Canadian and Australian authorities and the Five Eyes agencies.
Migrating to post quantum cryptography will take years and must start now."},{"url":"https://ciome.economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/security/european-banks-under-pressure-as-ecb-sets-deadline-for-ai-cyber-risk-action-plans/132409474","title":"European banks under pressure as ecb sets deadline for ai ...","date":"2 days ago","content":"2 days ago — The ECB has directed European banks to submit comprehensive AI-related cyber risk action plans by October, reflecting heightened regulatory ...Read more"}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"ECB banks instruction AI-related cyber collapse risk planning expectations","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://postquantum.com/security-pqc/ecb-ai-cybersecurity-letter","title":"ECB AI Cybersecurity Letter to Banks: Quantum Is Next","content":"# The ECB’s AI Cybersecurity Letter to Banks Is Also a Quantum Announcement. **July 7, 2026** — The European Central Bank published a letter from Claudia Buch, Chair of its Supervisory Board, to the chief executives of the roughly 110 significant institutions it directly supervises. The instruction is concrete: assess the new threat environment without delay, then submit a comprehensive action plan, with named owners, allocated resources, and implementation timelines, to the bank’s Joint Supervisory Team (JST) by October 31, 2026. The ECB’s AI cybersecurity letter is the first entry on its letters-to-banks page in more than four years. After three pages on artificial intelligence, Buch closes by noting that progress toward practical quantum computing will also reshape bank cybersecurity, that adoption of post-quantum cryptography (PQC) “must start now and necessitates sustained, strategic investment over time,” and that the ECB will address quantum risk to encryption in a separate letter in due course.","score":0.7892503,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.centralbanking.com/fintech/cyber/7976315/ecb-tells-banks-to-plan-for-ai-related-collapse-in-cyber-defences","title":"ECB tells banks to plan for AI-related collapse in cyber defences - Central Banking","content":"+ You are currently accessing Central Banking via your Enterprise account. If you already have an account please use the link below to sign in. If you have any problems with your access or would like to request an individual access account please contact our customer service team. Claudia Buch, chair of the European Central Bank’s supervisory board, has given banks until October 31 to submit their artificial intelligence security plans, shortly after the European Systemic Risk Board (ESRB) warned of the potential collapse of financial institutions’ cyber defences. In a letter to banks on July 7, Buch wrote that rapid advances in AI systems presented “pivotal changes to the cyber security landscape”. You are currently unable to print this content. If you already have an account, please sign in here.","score":0.78749067,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.linkedin.com/posts/bobchaput_ecb-tells-banks-to-bolster-ai-cyber-defences-activity-7480617944159502336-Z5CW","title":"ECB gives banks 4 months to address AI cyber threats","content":"The ECB's requirement for banks to develop AI cyber resilience plans illustrates a broader shift in regulatory expectations—from compliance","score":0.7813477,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.euronews.com/business/2026/07/07/ecb-tells-europes-biggest-banks-to-prepare-for-ai-powered-cyber-threats","title":"ECB tells Europe's biggest banks to prepare for AI-powered cyber threats | Euronews","content":"In each episode, two political heavyweights from across the EU face off to propose a diversity of opinions and spark conversations around the most important issues of EU affairs and the wider European political life. The eurozone's top banking supervisor, the ECB, on Tuesday told major European banks to draw up action plans to address cybersecurity risks posed by increasingly powerful artificial intelligence systems. The ECB's supervisory board sent a letter to the 110 banks it directly supervises, arguing that the latest AI models represent a \"long-term shift in the threat landscape rather than a temporary phenomenon\". The watchdog said frontier AI models represent a \"paradigm shift\" in cybersecurity and have become a source of systemic risk to the EU financial system. The ESRB warned that AI could dramatically reduce the time available for banks to identify and patch software vulnerabilities before they are exploited, increasing the risk of simultaneous cyber incidents across the financial sector.","score":0.69870013,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.americanbanker.com/news/ecb-tells-banks-fix-ai-cyber-gaps-by-oct-31","title":"ECB tells banks: Fix AI cyber gaps by Oct. 31 | American Banker","content":"# ECB tells banks: Fix AI cyber gaps by Oct. 31. * **Key insight**: Beyond faster patching, the letter tells banks to shrink their internet-facing attack surface, replace end-of-life technology and elevate cyber decisions to the board. * **What's at stake**: The euro-area subsidiaries of J.P. Morgan, Goldman Sachs, Citi and Morgan Stanley are ECB-supervised, so they face the same Oct. 31 deadline as any European bank. * **Forward look**: The ECB signals it is not done, saying a separate letter on the cyber risk from quantum computing is coming. The European Central Bank ordered the banks it supervises to draw up formal plans for defending against AI-powered cyberattacks and gave them until Oct. 31 to hand in those plans. The letter is one of the most detailed playbooks any major regulator has published on the cyber risks of frontier AI and a roadmap for U.S. supervisors if they decide to start a crackdown (although they have, to date, signaled no appetite for doing so).","score":0.67166495,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":0.87,"request_id":"3c10d8bd-82f1-4f0c-b9e3-e7690a204890"}
